behaviors.reasoning: Whether content is 'explicit' depends on the AI vision model's judgment — a mock always returns the same label, hiding real classification failures.
Detecting obfuscated hate speech (letter substitution, unicode lookalikes) requires real AI text model — deterministic rules miss novel obfuscation.
Publishing a passing post is deterministic pipeline wiring — if both checks pass, publish. No AI judgment involved in the publish decision itself.
5-second SLA is a latency/throughput constraint requiring real infrastructure under load — not about AI model quality.
API unavailability is a real network/service failure — testing the queue-for-retry path needs real or simulated service outage.
Consistency of a borderline AI classification requires the real model — a mock is deterministic by definition and always 'consistent'.
